threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 106 will get you from A to B in less than an hour. Welcome to Hackney chaos, mate.
Last night on the 59, a guy was singing opera to the driver. The whole bus joined in. Classic Brixton moment – you never know what you'll get!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a street performer doing a puppet show in front of a Kebab shop while a group of kids all chimed in, I knew this place has heart.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a U-Bahn station with an entire wall covered in graffiti art, showcasing local talent while waiting for your train. It's like a gallery underground.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission when they say ‘El Farolito’ with a knowing nod, while newcomers butcher the name like it’s a tongue twister.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a dude juggle on the 19 bus while two dogs barked at each other. Classic San Francisco. Only here would you find street performers above the napkin holders.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the 'local' shops in Gràcia are for them. Most locals avoid the main streets; we know the hidden gems off the beaten path.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still struggle with the metro map—missing the stops, looking lost, even at regular hours.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still cross the road when you see a bus coming, not realizing it’ll just stop for you at the next stop.
Caught a night bus home from Brixton last week—two guys were arguing over the best way to properly pronounce 'Sainsbury's'. Classic South London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was home when I heard a tattooed guy serenading the Kranoldplatz pigeons with an accordion at dawn last week.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a public park that doubles as a massive open-air club at night—nothing like learning to dance under the trees.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate BART delays like they’re just another Tuesday. It’s the eye-roll and shrug combo that gives it away.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus where the driver was belting out Whitney Houston. Random, but for a moment, it felt like a concert on wheels. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: You know that fancy Gaudí house? Most locals avoid it like the plague. We’d rather wander the side streets for real tapas and local chatter.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still stand awkwardly at the bus stop, consulting their phones while locals seamlessly navigate the timetable boards.
